case.question: Trace the downward path starting from the number '1' at the top. Rule: You must travel continuously downwards, but every time you encounter a horizontal line (rung), you must cross it to the adjacent vertical line before continuing downwards. Which letter at the bottom do you reach?
case.rubric: Starting at 1, the path travels down line 1, crosses the first rung to line 2, goes down line 2 (bypassing rung 2 which is on lines 3-4), crosses the third rung to line 3, and continues straight down line 3 (bypassing rung 4 which is on lines 1-2) to end at C.

planner_focus.weakness: following one continuous path through crossings, switches, occlusions, or near-overlaps

case.planner_response:
```json
{
  "slug": "amidakuji_routing_ladder",
  "type": "Visual Tracking",
  "subtype": "Line or route tracking",
  "question": "Trace the downward path starting from the number '1' at the top. Rule: You must travel continuously downwards, but every time you encounter a horizontal line (rung), you must cross it to the adjacent vertical line before continuing downwards. Which letter at the bottom do you reach?",
  "answer": "C",
  "image_prompt": "A clean, minimalist vector graphic of an Amidakuji (ladder) logic puzzle on a white background. Exactly 4 straight vertical lines evenly spaced, drawn in crisp black. The top ends are labeled with the numbers '1', '2', '3', '4' from left to right. The bottom ends are labeled with the letters 'A', 'B', 'C', 'D' from left to right. Exactly 4 horizontal line segments (rungs) connect adjacent vertical lines. Rung 1 connects line 1 and line 2 at 15% from the top. Rung 2 connects line 3 and line 4 at 35% from the top. Rung 3 connects line 2 and line 3 at 60% from the top. Rung 4 connects line 1 and line 2 at 85% from the top. All lines are solid black. No other text or background clutter. (Note: The correct destination starting from 1 is C. Distractor destinations are A, B, and D. It is critical to place the rungs exactly at the specified heights and connections so the visual route logic holds.)",
  "rubric": "Starting at 1, the path travels down line 1, crosses the first rung to line 2, goes down line 2 (bypassing rung 2 which is on lines 3-4), crosses the third rung to line 3, and continues straight down line 3 (bypassing rung 4 which is on lines 1-2) to end at C."
}
```
case.prompt: Create one BabyVision-style visual logical puzzle image. Use a clean printed worksheet style on a white background, crisp geometric shapes, small labels, no photorealism, no watermark, and no explanatory answer key. It must be unambiguous and solvable from the image alone. Do not include the written question in the image. A clean, minimalist vector graphic of an Amidakuji (ladder) logic puzzle on a white background. Exactly 4 straight vertical lines evenly spaced, drawn in crisp black. The top ends are labeled with the numbers '1', '2', '3', '4' from left to right. The bottom ends are labeled with the letters 'A', 'B', 'C', 'D' from left to right. Exactly 4 horizontal line segments (rungs) connect adjacent vertical lines. Rung 1 connects line 1 and line 2 at 15% from the top. Rung 2 connects line 3 and line 4 at 35% from the top. Rung 3 connects line 2 and line 3 at 60% from the top. Rung 4 connects line 1 and line 2 at 85% from the top. All lines are solid black. No other text or background clutter. (Note: The correct destination starting from 1 is C. Distractor destinations are A, B, and D. It is critical to place the rungs exactly at the specified heights and connections so the visual route logic holds.)
